content.description:
# Hob View Conversion Tracker

Tracking progress of converting JS views to Hob on the `hob-views` branch.

## North Star

Hob should be the obvious choice for *any* user-programming within Hobson. There should be no reason to choose JS except for familiarity. Hob — and its integration with Hobson (item references, code-as-data, structural editing, queryable AST) — should be better than JS for most things, and as good for the rest.

This reframes the remaining work. The goal is not "add enough primitives to convert the remaining views" — it's to make Hob genuinely superior for Hobson programming. Every primitive should be designed so that Hob is what you'd choose even if you knew JS perfectly.

## Converted (19/41)

- sortable-list-view
- note-widget-view
- widget-markdown-view
- event-definition-view
- item-search-view
- error-view
- inline-card-view
- compact-card-view
- related-items-view
- element-inspector-view
- tag-browser-view
- error-list-view
- symbol-browser-view
- widget-slider-view *(first view using pageContext via invoke)*
- widget-radio-group-view
- system-browser-view *(first complex multi-pane view; exercises persistent view state via get-view-config/update-view-config!, self-owned atom pattern)*
- modal-frame-view *(first view using sub-item rendering + document event lifecycle; exercises render-item options map, on-document!, on-cleanup!, get-sibling-container)*
- viewport-view *(largest view converted; exercises on-event!, render-item with decorator/on-cycle, context menu library orchestration via invoke, URL parameter parsing, silent save via invoke api)*
- spatial-canvas-view *(most complex conversion; exercises selector-filtered reactivity, pmap, 5 JS library orchestration via invoke/dom-set!, nested render-item with sibling-container. Conversion uncovered 3 systemic fixes: morphdom handler retention, selector pins, bare dep fallback)*

## Remaining — Reassessed

With persistent view state, sub-item rendering, and document events all implemented, many previously-blocked views are now convertible. Updated blocking status below.

### Ready to convert (no new primitives needed)

| View | Lines | Notes |
|------|------:|-------|
| hobson-instance-view | 147 | DOM ops + invoke for iframe history patching |
| type-workshop-view | 750 | Persistent state implemented; dynamic forms use existing DOM ops |

### Needs small additions

| View | Lines | Remaining gap |
|------|------:|------|
| app-page-view | 374 | pageContext shared state pattern, design mode library integration |
| test-suite-view | 295 | Promise orchestration, js-eval/Function access, silent save, heavy callback patterns |

### Staying JS

| View | Notes |
|------|-------|
| kernel:default-view | System fallback, must stay JS until Hob is fully trusted |

## Skipped — JS-native (for now)

These views exist to execute user-specified JS code (`new Function(...)`) and would gain nothing from Hob conversion today. Once Hob is the default user-programming language, inline JS execution becomes the exception rather than the norm — these views may eventually become Hob-native too.

- widget-button-view — executes inline JS on click
- script-view — displays and runs JS code items
- widget-checkbox-group-view — executes user JS to load options, unused, poor design-mode UX

## Skipped — Trivial generic-view wrappers

~12 small views (2-10 lines) that just delegate to generic-view. Not worth converting — no logic to exercise.

## Where Hob is already better than JS

- **Item references** (`@my-note`) are first-class, not string constants
- **Domain ops** (`get-field`, `set-field!`, `navigate!`) express Hobson concepts directly
- **Homoiconicity** — macros can transform code structurally
- **Sandboxed evaluation** — the system can reason about what code does

## Roadmap

### 1. JSON AST as canonical representation (implemented)

See: [JSON AST as Canonical Code Representation](c0c0c0c0-0051-0000-0000-000000000000)

Hob code is now stored as compact JSON AST (`content.hob`), making it queryable without parsing, structurally editable, and inspectable with the same tools as any item property. The evaluator dispatches directly on compact JSON types — no parsing step. Phase 1 migrated all Hob items to JSON AST; Phase 2 eliminated the `expandCompact` bridge entirely.

- Unlocks: structural editor, queryable code, safe macro expansion

### 2. Persistent view state (implemented)

Local mutable state via atoms (with self-owned atom pattern to prevent re-render loops) plus `get-view-config`/`update-view-config!` for state that persists across re-renders. The self-owned atom fix tags atoms with their creation tracking context so `reset!` on view-local atoms doesn't trigger full re-evaluation. `updateViewConfig` saves silently to avoid reactivity cascades.

- Unlocks: ~~type-workshop~~, ~~system-browser~~, ~~viewport~~, ~~spatial-canvas~~

### 3. Sub-item rendering (implemented)

`render-item` now accepts an options map (`:view`, `:sibling-container`, `:on-cycle`, `:decorator`) forwarded to the rendering system. `get-sibling-container` exposes the parent's sibling container for wrapping patterns (close-then-delegate). First exercised by modal-frame-view.

- Unlocks: ~~viewport~~, app-page, ~~spatial-canvas~~, ~~modal-frame~~

### 4. Document-level events with cleanup (implemented)

`on-document!` registers a document event listener and auto-removes it when the view's DOM is cleaned up. Returns a removal function for explicit unsubscription. `on-cleanup!` registers arbitrary cleanup handlers wired to the DOM cleanup protocol. Cleanup handlers are bundled onto the root DOM node by `renderHobView`.

- Unlocks: ~~viewport~~, ~~modal-frame~~

### 5. Reactivity / dependency tracking (implemented — basic + selector-filtered)

The `RenderInstanceRegistry` indexes every rendered instance by `itemId`, `viewId`, and `parentId`. When items change (via `item:updated` events), the registry looks up affected instances and schedules re-renders. Selector-filtered reactivity (`get-item :select`) lets Hob views declare which data slice they care about — re-renders fire only when the selector output changes (compared via `deepEquals`).

**Selector-filtered reactivity** (Feb 2026): `(get-item id :select fn)` extracts the slice a view cares about. Re-renders only fire when that slice changes (via `deepEquals`). This is how spatial-canvas-view avoids re-rendering on drag/resize — the structural selector only watches child count and minimize/maximize/anchor state.

**Selector pins**: once a selector is registered for a (context, itemId) pair, later bare `get-item` calls on the same item don't widen it — the selector is the intentional reactivity contract.

**Bare dep fallback**: `renderHobView` registers an implicit bare dep on item.id only if the view didn't register its own dep during evaluation. Views with structural selectors are not overridden.

- Unlocks: ~~spatial-canvas~~ (selector-filtered), efficient nested rendering

### 6. Structural editor (Phases 1–6 complete)

Enabled by JSON AST. The strongest UX argument for Hob over JS — edit code structure directly, no syntax errors possible, the system can assist meaningfully because it understands what you're editing.

Implemented in `field-view-hob-structural` + `hob-structural-css`:
- Phase 1: Navigation & display with syntax colouring, pretty-printing, breadcrumbs
- Phase 2: Holes, input/string modes, token resolution, undo/redo, save
- Phase 3: Wrap, unwrap, move, slurp, barf, duplicate, change container type
- Phase 4: Inline autocomplete (symbol prefix-match + `@`-triggered async item search via `item-search-lib`)
- Phase 5: Mouse interactions (double-click to edit, click between siblings to insert)
- Phase 6: Scope-aware autocomplete (binding/param/def analysis, priority ordering with detail labels)

Remaining (Phase 7 — polish): copy/paste, context-sensitive hints, comment support, keyboard help overlay.
